questions.question: A car travels at a speed of 40 mph over a certain distance and then returns over the same distance at a speed of 60 mph. What is the average speed for the total journey?
The philosopher's ___ arguments were extremely difficult for students to comprehend.
What is the difference between method overloading and method overriding?

answers.A2: Overloading: same class, different params; Overriding: inheritance, same signature
answers.A3: Overriding: same class, different params; Overloading: inheritance, same signature
answers.A4: Overloading uses virtual functions; Overriding doesn't

questions.key: Overloading: same class, different params; Overriding: inheritance, same signature
questions.justification: Overloading is compile-time polymorphism in the same class with different parameters. Overriding is runtime polymorphism across inheritance hierarchy with same signature.

questions.question: What is the Interface Segregation Principle?

answers.A2: Clients should not depend on interfaces they don't use
answers.A3: Classes must implement all interface methods
answers.A4: Interfaces should be as large as possible

questions.key: Clients should not depend on interfaces they don't use
questions.justification: Interfaces should be small and focused, with clients implementing only the methods they need.

questions.question: What is a const_cast in C++?

answers.A1: A cast that makes everything constant
answers.A2: A cast that adds or removes const qualifier
answers.A3: A cast between const and non-const pointers/references

questions.key: A cast that adds or removes const qualifier
questions.justification: const_cast allows explicit conversion between const and non-const types, should be used carefully.
